name: Cursor IDE (rules, @ context, MCP host)
context_primitives: .cursor/rules with YAML frontmatter (alwaysApply, globs, descriptions) to inject stable instructions; @-mentions and editor selection to pull file/diff context; MCP client connects external tools and resources into the agent loop.
deployment_pattern: Commit rules to repo; configure MCP in Cursor settings; use scoped rules per subsystem to limit always-on tokens.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: N/A; product not benchmarked as a model. Effectiveness is measured by team productivity and defect rates.
ecosystem_integrations: MCP ecosystem, VS Code extension model, git worktrees, optional cloud models.
differentiators: IDE-native context (open files, linter diagnostics) plus first-class rules system aimed at reducing repeated natural-language instructions.
significance_for_builders: Split rules by concern and glob scope to avoid one giant system prompt; use MCP for data that should not be pasted into chat (tickets, DBs, internal APIs).
key_timeline_or_milestones: Docs describe migration from legacy .cursorrules to .cursor/rules modular layout.
